title: [Bug]: Subject-specific classification defaults accept control characters
summary: Subject-specific message metadata rules can set classification defaults that contain embedded control characters.
observed: MessageMetadataRuleConfig accepts a classification default containing an ASCII control character.
expected: Subject-specific priority and classification defaults should apply the same control-character rejection as global defaults.
reproduction: Construct MessageMetadataRuleConfig(subject='ops.>', classification='NATO SECRET\u0000') and observe validation succeeds.
failing_test: Add a unit test that expects a subject rule classification default with a control character to fail validation.
impact: Unsafe classification values can be applied to whole subject families and persisted into sinks.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. Invalid configuration should fail before the runner starts.
security: Classification values are security-relevant metadata and must be bounded, explicit, and free of control characters.
acceptance: Subject-specific priority and classification defaults reject all ASCII control characters.
The regression test fails before the fix and passes after the fix.
The operator-facing error describes invalid message metadata defaults.
No storage schema changes are introduced.
tests: Run focused metadata boundary tests and full validation.
documentation: Update release notes and sanitized test report.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized test evidence.
